data.useCase: Classify support tickets by issue type and urgency.
Classify support tickets by issue type and urgency.

message: creating search plan with OpenAI model gpt-5-mini

data:
Create a Hugging Face dataset search plan for post-training a language model.
Domain: customer support
Quality tier: 3
Use case: Classify support tickets by issue type and urgency.
Quality tier definitions:
1 = Fastest: under 10K rows, one tightly matched dataset, optimize for minutes to a few hours.
2 = Fast: 10K-50K rows, 1-2 complementary datasets, moderate filtering.
3 = Balanced: 50K-500K rows, 2-3 datasets, core use case plus edge cases.
4 = Quality: 500K-2M rows, 3-5 diverse high-quali...

analysis.domain_summary: Customer support domain: short-to-medium length texts (emails, ticket descriptions, chat logs) where labels are issue type (billing, technical, account, feature request, etc.) and urgency/severity (low/medium/high, SLA tiers). Useful datasets include labeled support tickets, annotated chat transcripts, and curated customer complaint corpora that include categorical issue labels and time-sensitive urgency tags. Expect class imbalance (more general inquiries than critical outages) and variable ...
analysis.quality_tier_strategy: Balanced (tier 3): target 50K–500K total rows by combining 2–3 complementary datasets. Prioritize one medium-sized, well-labeled core dataset that matches ticket/email/chat formats and label granularity, plus 1–2 smaller datasets to cover edge cases (escalations, legal, billing) and conversational formats. Plan for label schema harmonization, class rebalancing, and PII scrubbing. Use augmentation or synthetic examples only after assessing label quality.

search_queries: {"search":"customer support tickets","task_filter":"text-classification","sort":"downloads","min_rows":50000,"intent":"Find medium-to-large labeled ticket datasets (emails/ticket descriptions) suitable as a core dataset for issue-type classification."}

{"search":"support tickets labeled","task_filter":"text-classification","sort":"likes","min_rows":50000,"intent":"Locate datasets explicitly labeled for categories/issue types or urgency for direct supervision."}

{"search":"helpdesk tickets","task_filter":"text-classification","sort":"trending","min_rows":50000,"intent":"Discover helpdesk/IT ticket corpora that often include structured metadata (priority, status) useful for urgency labels."}

{"search":"technical support emails","task_filter":"text-classification","sort":"downloads","min_rows":50000,"intent":"Find email-based support datasets—longer text examples that help generalize across channels (email vs chat)."}

{"search":"support conversations","task_filter":"conversational","sort":"likes","min_rows":50000,"intent":"Locate chat transcripts or conversational logs that can be converted to turn-level or ticket-level classification data."}

{"search":"customer complaints","task_filter":"text-classification","sort":"created","min_rows":50000,"intent":"Identify complaint-focused corpora that surface high-severity/urgent cases and diverse issue types."}

{"search":"issue classification dataset","task_filter":"text-classification","sort":"downloads","min_rows":50000,"intent":"Search for datasets explicitly framed as issue/incident classification to maximize label relevance."}

{"search":"ticket urgency labels","task_filter":"text-classification","sort":"likes","min_rows":50000,"intent":"Find datasets that include explicit priority/urgency annotations to train the urgency dimension."}

{"search":"customer support dataset","task_filter":null,"sort":"downloads","min_rows":50000,"intent":"Catch multi-format or mixed-label datasets (conversations, tickets, emails) that might require conversion but offer volume."}
ranking_criteria: {"criterion":"Label relevance and granularity","weight":"high","description":"Does the dataset include explicit issue-type and urgency/priority labels, or easily mappable fields (e.g., priority, category)? Prefer datasets whose label set maps closely to your taxonomy."}

{"criterion":"Effective dataset size and class balance","weight":"high","description":"Total rows and distribution across classes—choose datasets with sufficient examples per class or plan to combine datasets/augment underrepresented classes."}

{"criterion":"Data format and channel match","weight":"medium","description":"Similarity to your target input (ticket descriptions, emails, chats). Conversational logs require different preprocessing than single-message tickets."}

{"criterion":"Annotation quality and provenance","weight":"medium","description":"Clear labeling methodology, human annotations vs heuristic labeling, and presence of metadata (timestamp, resolution) indicating reliable labels."}

{"criterion":"License and privacy risk","weight":"high","description":"Permissive license for commercial use and minimal legal/PII risk. Prioritize datasets with explicit licensing and documented PII handling."}

{"criterion":"Language and locale","weight":"medium","description":"Language(s) of the dataset and locale-specific terminology; prefer datasets matching your production language or plan for multilingual handling."}
{"criterion":"Duplication and dataset freshness","weight":"low","description":"Avoid redundant copies of the same corpus and prefer recent datasets if domain drift is a concern."}

recommendation_guidance.mixing_strategy: Select one primary medium-sized dataset (core ticket/email format) for label consistency and volume, then add 1–2 complementary datasets: one conversational/chat dataset and one smaller edge-case dataset (escalations, legal, billing). Harmonize label schemas into a unified taxonomy, resample or augment to reduce class imbalance, and apply PII redaction and deduplication. Validate label mapping on a held-out human-reviewed subset before full training.
recommendation_guidance.warnings: ["Labels vary across datasets—expect to remap disparate taxonomies and resolve coarse vs fine-grained labels.","Class imbalance: critical/urgent cases are often rare and may require targeted augmentation or upsampling.","PII risk: customer support data frequently contains personal data; plan robust redaction and review licensing for sensitive content.","Channel mismatch: combining emails, tickets, and chat logs can introduce format noise—normalize fields and consider separate preprocessing.",...

Infer a deterministic normalization recipe for a single Hugging Face dataset so a text-only SFT backend can train on it.
Do not invent columns.
Use the native dataset field names exactly as provided.
Return usable=false when the schema cannot support a meaningful deterministic text or prompt/completion normalization.
Use template_synthesis only when direct column mapping is impossible.
Avoid likely identifier columns such as ticket ids, customer ids, phone numbers, and other obvious PII unles...

data:
Choose the smallest useful set of already-fetched Hugging Face datasets for the user's post-training use case.
Use only the provided candidates. Do not invent new dataset ids.
Return 1 dataset when a single clear winner is enough.
Return 2-3 datasets only when combining them would materially improve coverage, robustness, or task fit.
Do not return more than 3 datasets.
Prefer domain relevance, task/schema fit, target row-band fit, public usability, license clarity, and overall data usefulness...
